title: 10 best herbal creams for hangover

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with hangover because they contain powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can alleviate symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea. The creams are also rich in flavonoids, which help to neutralize toxins and soothe the digestive system. Additionally, maidenhair tree extracts have been shown to possess adaptogenic properties, helping the body to recover from the stress of overindulgence by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation. This natural formula can provide quick relief from hangover symptoms, allowing you to bounce back faster than ever.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maidenhair tree cream contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, leading to a faster recovery.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in the cream can help alleviate headaches, fatigue, and nausea associated with hangovers by reducing inflammation in the body.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: The moisturizing effects of the cream can help soothe dry skin, irritated throats, and dehydrated bodies, while its soothing properties can calm upset stomachs and reduce stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help soothe and calm the skin irritated by hangover symptoms.
Barks: Containing astringent properties, the barks help constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of beeswax, 50g of coconut oil, and 20g of shea butter for the base cream ingredients.
Infuse 10g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 100ml of warm olive oil for 4 hours to create a potent extract.
Melt the base cream ingredients together in a double boiler and stir until fully combined taking approximately 15 minutes.
Add 5g of the ginkgo biloba extract to the melted base cream mixture and mix well ensuring thorough incorporation.
Pour the final cream mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for about an hour before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with hangover because they contain soothing properties that alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and digestive discomfort. The menthol in peppermint oil has a natural ability to calm the stomach and ease digestive issues, reducing inflammation and cramps caused by excessive drinking. Additionally, peppermint's refreshing aroma can help clear mental fog and promote relaxation, helping to reduce anxiety and stress associated with hangovers.
remedy_properties: Digestive Soothing Properties: Peppermint cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds can help calm nausea, bloating, and stomach discomfort caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

Pain Relief Properties: The menthol and other volatile oils in peppermint cream have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can ease headaches, muscle soreness, and joint pain associated with hangover.

Dehydration Relief Properties: Peppermint's cooling and refreshing properties can help replenish lost fluids and electrolytes, alleviating symptoms like dry mouth, fatigue, and dizziness that often accompany hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Rhyzomes: Known for their digestive properties, rhyzomes can help ease nausea and stomach upset associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Gather organic peppermint leaves fresh or dried according to your preference.
Combine 20g of dried peppermint leaves with 100ml of boiling water for 10 minutes steeping time.
Strain the mixture and add 30g of beeswax pellets and 20g of coconut oil stirring until melted.
Allow the mixture to cool down slightly before whipping it with a hand mixer for 2 minutes.
Transfer the cream into a clean glass jar and store in refrigerator at 4c for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with hangover because they provide rapid relief from nausea, dizziness, and stomach discomfort caused by excessive alcohol consumption. The natural anti-inflammatory properties of ginger help to reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating headaches and soreness. Additionally, ginger's digestive enzymes aid in breaking down fats and proteins, easing indigestion and bloating. By soothing the stomach and reducing inflammation, herbal ginger creams provide a gentle and effective way to alleviate hangover symptoms, allowing you to recover quickly and feel refreshed.
remedy_properties: Gingerol: Gingerol, a bioactive compound found in ginger, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nausea and headaches associated with hangover symptoms.

Shogaol: Shogaol, another active compound in ginger, has been shown to have antacid properties, which can help neutralize stomach acid and reduce heartburn and digestive discomfort often experienced during a hangover.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in herbal ginger creams, such as citral and geraniol, possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nausea and headaches caused by hangovers.
Roots: Roots contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which are known for their anti-nausea and pain-relieving effects.
remedy_recipe: Extract fresh ginger root zingiber officinale using 200ml of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.
Add 20g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil to the infused ginger liquid heating until melted and combined.
Stir in 5ml of lavender essential oil and 2ml of peppermint essential oil allowing mixture to cool and thicken.
Pour the cooled cream into a sterilized container and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with hangover because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation in the body. After a night of heavy drinking, the liver can become overwhelmed and release toxins into the bloodstream, leading to inflammation and discomfort. Curcumin in turmeric creams works to neutralize these toxins and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall recovery and reducing hangover severity.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
Gingerol and Shogaol: These active compounds in ginger have natural anti-nausea and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in relieving hangover-induced stomach upset and discomfort.

Essential oils (e.g., peppermint, lavender): The calming and soothing properties of these oils can help ease headaches, fatigue, and irritability associated with hangovers by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce hangover symptoms such as nausea and headaches.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including those caused by excessive alcohol consumption, making them effective in soothing hangover stomachaches.
remedy_recipe: Extract 2 teaspoons of dried turmeric root powder from curcuma longa using 4 ounces of coconut oil at room temperature for 2 hours.
Combine the extracted mixture with 1 teaspoon of beeswax and 1/4 teaspoon of vitamin e oil, heating gently until melted and smooth.
Add 10 drops of peppermint essential oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture, stirring well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Store the finished cream in an airtight container at a temperature below 70 degrees fahrenheit, using within 6 weeks of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with hangover because of its calming and soothing properties. The herb's essential oils, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort often associated with hangovers. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects can help alleviate dryness and itchiness caused by excessive drinking. By applying the cream topically, individuals may experience relief from headache symptoms, nausea, and overall fatigue, allowing them to feel more refreshed and revitalized after a night of indulgence.
remedy_properties: Calming and Anti-Anxiety Effects: Lavender's calming effects can help soothe the emotional and psychological symptoms of a hangover, such as anxiety and irritability, by reducing stress hormones like cortisol.

Digestive Soothing Properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties may help alleviate digestive issues caused by excessive drinking, such as stomach cramps, nausea, and vomiting, by relaxing the muscles in the gut and reducing inflammation.

Pain-Relieving and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Lavender's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce hangover-related pain, such as headaches and muscle aches, by blocking pain pathways and reducing swelling and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate hangover symptoms like headache and nausea.
Buds: Containing essential oils with calming and sedative effects, the buds help reduce anxiety and stress associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine the lavender powder with 50ml of coconut oil in a heatproof glass bowl and place it in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the coconut oil with the lavender properties.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before adding 20% distilled water and mixing well.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized glass jars and seal them tightly; store in a dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams helps with hangover because they contain parthenolide, a natural compound that reduces inflammation and alleviates pain. When applied topically to the skin, feverfew cream can help alleviate headaches, which are often a major symptom of hangovers. Additionally, feverfew's anti-inflammatory properties may also help soothe stomach discomfort and nausea commonly associated with hangover syndrome. By targeting these symptoms, feverfew creams provide effective relief from the misery of a hangover.
remedy_properties: Pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew contains parthenolide, which helps to reduce headache and body ache caused by hangover, as well as swelling and inflammation in the brain and body.

Antioxidant properties: Feverfew has antioxidant properties that help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by alcohol consumption, reducing the severity of hangover symptoms such as nausea, fatigue, and dizziness.

Digestive soothing compounds: Feverfew contains flavonoids and terpenes that can help soothe digestive issues like nausea, vomiting, and stomach discomfort caused by excessive drinking, allowing for a smoother recovery from hangover.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help alleviate symptoms such as nausea and vomiting often associated with hangovers.
Leaves: Containing parthenolide, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing hangover-related headaches.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including dried tanacetum parthenium leaves 10 grams, coconut oil 50 milliliters, beeswax 20 grams, and vitamin e oil 5 drops.
Combine dried tanacetum parthenium leaves with coconut oil in a heat-resistant container and steep for 2 hours under the sun or using a warm water bath.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding solids and reserving the infused oil.
Melt beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl at 30-second intervals, stirring between each interval until fully liquefied, then add vitamin e oil and the reserved infused oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use, taking approximately 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with hangover because its soothing and calming properties help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, alleviating symptoms such as headaches and fatigue. The cream's anti-inflammatory agents also work to ease stomach upset and nausea, while its antimicrobial properties help to combat the negative effects of alcohol on the skin. Additionally, chamomile's natural relaxing properties can help to calm an anxious or irritable mood, promoting a sense of well-being and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which may alleviate headaches and fatigue caused by a hangover.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants can neutralize free radicals and protect the body from oxidative stress, which may help mitigate the damage caused by alcohol consumption and reduce hangover symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.

Calming and soothing effects: Chamomile's calming and soothing compounds can help relax muscles, ease anxiety and irritability, and promote a sense of calmness, making it easier to recover from a night of heavy drinking.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile roots can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with hangovers.
Seeds: Containing high levels of antioxidants and fatty acids, chamomile seeds can provide nourishment and hydration to parched skin, helping to combat dehydration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and sort them from any debris.
Combine 20g of matricaria chamomilla flowers with 100ml of distilled water in a heatproof glass measuring cup.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-resistant bowl set over a pot of simmering water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Mix 30ml of the infused liquid with 50g of emulsifying wax and 20g of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant bowl, heating until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams help with hangover because they contain hyperforin, a natural compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to areas such as the temples and forehead, hyperforin works to reduce inflammation and tension caused by excessive alcohol consumption, alleviating symptoms like headaches and fatigue. Additionally, St John's Wort creams may also help to regulate serotonin levels in the brain, which can aid in reducing nausea and dizziness associated with hangovers.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This active compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce headaches, fatigue, and irritability associated with hangovers.

Flavonoids: St John's Wort creams contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals in the body, reducing oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

Omega-7 fatty acids: These fatty acids have anti-inflammatory and regenerative properties, helping to soothe and repair damaged tissues in the skin and mucous membranes, alleviating hangover-related discomforts like nausea and mouth dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hypericin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce hangover symptoms.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids and flavonoids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate hangover discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves from a trusted source or purchase dried herb in bulk weighing 20 grams for the recipe.
Combine the harvested or dried st john's wort with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and let it steep for four to six hours under warm sunlight.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container pressing on solids to extract oils and resins from the herb.
Add one teaspoon of beeswax per 20 grams of infused oil to emulsify the cream and heat it gently over low heat with constant stirring until melted completely.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers or glass jars and let it cool and set for at least two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with hangover because they contain natural sedative properties that can help alleviate symptoms of a hangover, such as headaches, fatigue, and insomnia. The cream's active ingredients, including valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and irritability often associated with a hangover. Additionally, valerian's ability to promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension can help alleviate body aches and stiffness, making it easier to recover from a night out.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian root's sedative properties help to reduce stress, anxiety, and irritability often associated with hangovers, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities: Valerian's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds can help mitigate the oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing symptoms like headaches and fatigue.

Digestive soothing properties: Valerian root has natural anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects that can ease nausea, cramps, and digestive discomfort often experienced after a night of heavy drinking.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, which helps to calm nervous system and reduce symptoms of hangover.
Rhizomes: Contain sesquiterpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing hangover-induced pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots and dry them thoroughly in a cool dark place for at least seven days.
Grind the dried valerian roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the valerian root powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat.
Stir the mixture constantly for approximately 30 minutes or until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth and creamy.
Pour the cooled herbal cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with hangover because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in reducing the negative effects of excessive alcohol consumption. The cream's dandelion extract helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the cream help to neutralize the oxidative stress caused by alcohol, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the duration of hangover symptoms. This natural remedy provides a gentle and soothing relief, making it an effective and healthy solution for hangover treatment.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion contains antioxidants like flavonoids and terpenes, which help neutralize free radicals in the body, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

Diuretic properties: The diuretic compounds in dandelion, such as taraxasterol, can help increase urine production, flushing out toxins and excess fluids from the body, alleviating dehydration and headache symptoms commonly associated with hangovers.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, including sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, may help reduce inflammation and pain in the liver, stomach, and other affected areas, easing discomfort and promoting a smoother recovery from hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to possess anti-hangover properties, including reducing nausea, headaches, and liver damage.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 200g of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and grind into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground taraxacum officinale powder with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with the herbal extract.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids, then add 10% of beeswax by weight to thicken the cream.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with hangover because they contain compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, soothing stomach cramps and nausea. The herbs in lemon balm also help to reduce inflammation and ease headaches, which are common symptoms of a hangover. Additionally, lemon balm's antispasmodic properties can help to alleviate digestive issues such as bloating and indigestion, making it a natural remedy for alleviating the discomforts of a morning after a night out.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption, which can alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream may help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often exacerbated during a hangover, and ease symptoms like nausea and dizziness.

Gastroprotective properties: Lemon balm's gastroprotective effects can help soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and stomach discomfort that are common after excessive drinking.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin during hangover.
Seeds: Lemon balm seeds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and promote detoxification in the body after a night of excessive drinking.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Blend one tablespoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves with two tablespoons of coconut oil in a small bowl.
Add one teaspoon of beeswax and heat the mixture gently over low heat for 10-15 minutes stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter to remove any solids and discard the solids.
Pour the liquid into a sterilized glass jar and let it cool and set at room temperature for at least 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with hangover because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach. After a night of excessive drinking, the body can become inflamed and irritated, leading to symptoms like nausea and headaches. The anti-inflammatory properties of glycyrrhizin in licorice creams help to alleviate these symptoms, providing relief from hangover discomfort. Additionally, the creams' natural sweetness can also help to settle an upset stomach and calm the nervous system.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as headache, nausea, and fatigue associated with a hangover.
Saponins: Act as a natural anti-nausea agent, helping to soothe an upset stomach and reduce vomiting and diarrhea caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Flavonoids: Possess antioxidant properties that help to neutralize the damage caused by free radicals in the body, which can exacerbate hangover symptoms such as fatigue, weakness, and brain fog.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea associated with hangovers.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing the digestive system during a hangover.
Seeds: The seeds are rich in glycosides, which help to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried licorice root (glycyrrhiza glabra) into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered licorice root with beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax melts completely within 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat, let it cool slightly before whipping the mixture into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 5-7 minutes.
Fill small containers with the herbal cream and store in the refrigerator at 39°f to 46°f (4°c to 8°c) for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with hangover because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the digestive system and reduce nausea. The antioxidants in sage also help to neutralize the harmful toxins produced by excessive alcohol consumption, which can exacerbate hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue. Additionally, sage has a natural calming effect on the mind and body, which can help to alleviate feelings of anxiety and stress often associated with hangovers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Sage creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can alleviate hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sage creams, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hangovers, including nausea, stomach upset, and joint pain.

Digestive Soothing Properties: Sage's carnosic acid has been shown to have gastroprotective effects, which can help alleviate digestive issues like stomach cramps, diarrhea, and vomiting that often accompany hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing the digestive system and reducing inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain salicin, a natural pain reliever that helps alleviate headaches and body aches commonly associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and dry them in a dark place for 2 weeks to preserve their potency.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried salvia officinalis into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered salvia officinalis with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10 minutes until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 180°f.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with hangover because of its natural properties that combat the symptoms associated with excessive alcohol consumption. The herb's ability to improve circulation and relieve mental fatigue can help alleviate headaches and dizziness caused by dehydration. Additionally, rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce swelling in the brain and body, which can contribute to nausea and stomach discomfort often experienced during a hangover. By applying rosemary creams topically, individuals can potentially experience faster relief from these symptoms, helping to ease the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Rosemary creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the brain and body caused by excessive drinking, alleviating symptoms like headache and fatigue.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of rosemary cream can help neutralize free radicals produced during alcohol metabolism, reducing oxidative stress and damage to cells and tissues, which can contribute to hangover symptoms.
Digestive stimulant effects: Rosemary's digestive stimulant properties can help stimulate digestion and relieve nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain often experienced after excessive drinking.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation caused by hangovers.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, barks have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as nausea and stomach pain, common symptoms of hangover.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried leaves of rosmarinus officinalis in bulk for subsequent infusion.
Prepare emulsifying base by mixing 1/4 cup of coconut oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Add soothing agents to enhance hangover relief and skin health.
Fill and seal sterilized jars with the cream mixture, ensuring airtight containers.
Allow cream to cool and thicken at room temperature before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with hangover because it contains natural anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate the painful symptoms of a hangover, such as headaches and fatigue. The antioxidants present in thyme also help to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to the damage caused by excessive drinking. Additionally, thyme's digestive benefits can soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea, allowing you to recover faster from a night out.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can alleviate hangover symptoms like fatigue and nausea.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hangovers.

Digestive soothing properties: The mucilages in thyme cream can help protect and heal the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by excessive drinking, and promoting a smoother recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate hangover symptoms like pain and swelling.
Buds: Thyme buds contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-nausea and anti-vomiting effects, making them effective for treating hangover-related stomach issues.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 20g of dried thymus vulgaris, 100ml of sweet almond oil and 50ml of beeswax.
Combine the dried thymus vulgaris with 50ml of hot water in a saucepan and allow it to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids.
Add the sweet almond oil and beeswax to the liquid mixture, heating gently until the beeswax has melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for about an hour before refrigerating or storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with hangover because they contain natural ingredients that combat the unpleasant symptoms of a night out. The bitter compounds in the cream, such as naringenin and aurantiamarin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea, dizziness, and fatigue caused by excessive alcohol consumption. Additionally, the soothing properties of the cream can help calm digestive issues, making it an effective way to ease hangover symptoms and get back on track after a big night out.
remedy_properties: Quercetin: A flavonoid antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, leading to relief from hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Naringenin: A flavanone present in bitter orange peel that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset often experienced after a night of heavy drinking.

Vitamin C: Present in the citrus peels used to make herbal bitter orange creams, vitamin C acts as an antioxidant and helps to neutralize free radicals formed during alcohol metabolism, reducing oxidative stress and potentially alleviating hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, bitter orange roots help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting digestive health.
Barks: Containing a unique blend of tannins and phenolic compounds, bitter orange barks provide antioxidant properties that combat oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried citrus x aurantium peel and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered citrus x aurantium peel with 250 milliliters of fractionated coconut oil in a double boiler setup for gentle heating.
Simmer the mixture at low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to allow for effective infusion and extraction.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids for further processing.
Allow the mixture to cool completely then whip it with an immersion blender or whisk until it thickens into a creamy consistency suitable for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with hangover because of its unique combination of properties that address multiple symptoms. The cream's anti-inflammatory agents help to soothe aching heads, while its refreshing aroma invigorates and uplifts the senses. Additionally, lemongrass contains natural antioxidants that help to combat oxidative stress caused by excessive drinking, reducing feelings of fatigue and nausea. As a result, using an herbal lemongrass cream can provide instant relief from hangover symptoms, helping individuals recover faster and feel more refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lemongrass contains anti-inflammatory compounds like citral and geraniol, which help reduce swelling and pain in the body, making it effective in alleviating hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich components of lemongrass cream help neutralize free radicals produced during heavy drinking, reducing oxidative stress and damage to cells, which can contribute to hangover symptoms.

Digestive soothing agents: Lemongrass contains compounds that have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to alleviate nausea, vomiting, and stomach upset often experienced after excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing hangover symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contains citral, a natural analgesic that helps alleviate headaches and nausea associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and chop them into small pieces using scissors in about 5 minutes.
Combine the chopped cymbopogon citratus leaves with 50 milliliters of boiling water to make a herbal infusion, allowing it to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the herbal infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solid particles, retaining the liquid in about 5 minutes.
Mix the filtered herbal infusion with 200 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax, heating the mixture gently until the beeswax melts completely after 10 minutes.
Pour the melted mixture into a clean glass container, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for about 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Galangal creams helps with hangover because it contains active compounds that alleviate symptoms of a morning after. The anti-inflammatory properties in galangal reduce swelling and soothe headaches, while its antispasmodic effects ease nausea and stomach cramps. Additionally, galangal's natural antimicrobial agents help to combat the negative impact of toxins on the body, promoting a faster recovery from excessive drinking.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Galangal's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce inflammation and alleviate headaches, nausea, and fatigue associated with hangovers.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in galangal cream may help neutralize the oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms like dizziness, weakness, and irritability.

Digestive-soothing properties: Galangal's digestive-friendly compounds, such as citral and geraniol, can help calm stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea caused by hangovers, promoting a sense of relief and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and fiber, galangal roots are used to absorb excess toxins from the body and reduce inflammation.
Rhizomes: With their natural antibacterial properties, galangal rhizomes help combat hangover-related infections and promote digestion.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20g of dried alpinia galanga root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar pestle.
Infuse 50ml of coconut oil with the powdered alpinia galanga in a double boiler for 30 minutes under gentle heat.
Combine the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl and melt under low heat stirring frequently.
Remove from heat and let cool to 60°c before whipping the mixture until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency using an electric whisk.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks or freeze for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with hangover because they contain the herb yarrow, which has been traditionally used to treat symptoms of excessive drinking. The cream's active compounds, such as chamazulene and apigenin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate hangover-related discomforts like headaches, nausea, and fatigue. By applying topically, these compounds can quickly absorb into the skin, providing rapid relief from hangover symptoms without having to ingest any harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort caused by excessive drinking, making it an effective natural remedy for hangover headaches.

Antioxidant properties: Yarrow's antioxidants neutralize free radicals produced by ethanol metabolism, which can damage liver cells and contribute to hangover symptoms like fatigue, nausea, and dizziness.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Yarrow's vasoconstrictors help reduce blood vessel dilation caused by alcohol consumption, alleviating symptoms like headaches, facial flushing, and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of yarrow contain a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe hangover-related nausea and headaches.

Flowers: Yarrow flowers are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms such as inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers, ensuring freshness and potency.
Combine the dried herbs with 50 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container, steeping for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding solids immediately.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of coconut oil to the herbal infusion, melting completely over low heat for 10 minutes.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until creamy, then pour into sterilized containers, allowing to cool before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hangover are topical remedies infused with natural ingredients, such as arnica, peppermint, and ginger, which help alleviate symptoms associated with a night of excessive drinking. These creams work by reducing inflammation, soothing headaches, and calming nausea, allowing individuals to recover faster and feel more refreshed. For example, creams containing arnica can reduce swelling and bruising, while those with peppermint oil can ease stomach discomfort. By using herbal creams for hangover, individuals can quickly recover from a night out and get back to their daily routines, improving overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hangover with herbal creams is by applying a topical cream containing peppermint oil, ginger, and arnica. These ingredients help to alleviate nausea, reduce headache pain, and soothe inflammation. Massage the cream into your skin, focusing on areas such as the forehead, temples, and wrists. The anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs will work to ease discomfort and leave you feeling refreshed and revitalized.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hangover that are treated with herbal creams are Jet Lag, Fatigue, and Insomnia. Herbal creams containing ingredients like Ginkgo biloba, Valerian root, and Peppermint oil help alleviate symptoms of fatigue and jet lag by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Additionally, herbal creams infused with herbs like Chamomile and Lavender calm the mind and body, making them effective treatments for insomnia.
